nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hawkswell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Hauxwell
meanings: English: habitational name from Hauxwell in North Yorkshire, which derives its name from the Old English personal name Heafoc (genitive Heafoces) or the word heafoc ‘hawk’ (genitive heafoces) + wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Johanna de Hawkswell was born about 1296, in Hauxwell, Yorkshire, England, her father, Thomas de Hawkswell, was 19 and her mother, Cecily Scott, was 15. She married Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johanna de Hawkswell est née environ 1296, à Hauxwell, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Hawkswell, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Cecily Scott, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johanna de Hawkswell um 1296 in Hauxwell, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas de Hawkswell 19 und ihre Mutter Cecily Scott 15. Sie hat Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johanna de Hawkswell è nata il circa 1296 nel Hauxwell, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Thomas de Hawkswell, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Cecily Scott, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Johanna de Hawkswellが約1296年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハウクスウエルで生まれた時，父Thomas de Hawkswellは19歳，母Cecily Scottは15歳であった。Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna de Hawkswell(이)가 대략 1296년 영국 요르크실르 하스웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Hawkswell(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily Scott(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johanna de Hawkswell nasceu aproximadamente 1296, em Hauxwell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Hawkswell, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Cecily Scott, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johanna de Hawkswell родилась около 1296 г. в местности хауксвелл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Hawkswell было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily Scott, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johanna de Hawkswell nació aproximadamente 1296, en Hauxwell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Hawkswell, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Cecily Scott, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Geoffrey Pigot of Melmerby. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas.
